questions.q: If f(x) = sin^{-1}(x), then f(sin(π/6)) is?
questions.explanation: Since sin(π/6) is within the range of sin^{-1}x, we can directly substitute the value.

questions.q: If f(x) = tan^{-1}(x), then f(-x) is?
questions.explanation: Since the range of tan^{-1}x is restricted to (-π/2, π/2), we have to adjust the sign of the output.

questions.q: Which of the following is a property of inverse trigonometric functions?
questions.explanation: Inverse trigonometric functions are one-to-one functions, which is a necessary condition for them to be invertible.

questions.q: If f(x) = sin^{-1}x, then the domain of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The domain of sin^{-1}x is restricted to the interval [-1, 1] because the range of sinx is [-1, 1].
questions.topic: Domain and Range of Inverse Trigonometric Functions

questions.q: If f(x) = cos^{-1}x, then the range of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The range of cos^{-1}x is restricted to [0, π] because the range of cosx is [0, 1].
questions.topic: Domain and Range of Inverse Trigonometric Functions

questions.q: Which of the following is a composite function of inverse trigonometric functions?
questions.explanation: The composition of sin and cos^{-1}x is a composite function of inverse trigonometric functions.
questions.topic: Composite Functions of Inverse Trigonometric Functions

questions.q: If f(x) = tan^{-1}x, then the domain of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The domain of tan^{-1}x is restricted to (-∞, -1] ∪ [1, ∞) because the range of tanx is all real numbers.
questions.topic: Domain and Range of Inverse Trigonometric Functions

questions.q: If f(x) = sin^{-1}x, then the inverse function of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The inverse function of sin^{-1}x is sin(x).

questions.q: If f(x) = sin^{-1}x, then the derivative of f(x) is?
If f(x) = cos^{-1}x, then the derivative of f(x) is?
If f(x) = tan^{-1}x, then the derivative of f(x) is?
If f(x) = sin^{-1}x, then the integral of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The integral of sin^{-1}x is x sin^{-1}x + √(1-x^2).

questions.q: If f(x) = cos^{-1}x, then the integral of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The integral of cos^{-1}x is x cos^{-1}x - √(1-x^2).

questions.q: If f(x) = tan^{-1}x, then the integral of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The integral of tan^{-1}x is x tan^{-1}x - 1/2 ln(1+x^2).

questions.q: If f(x) = sin^{-1}x, then the second derivative of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The second derivative of sin^{-1}x is -x/√(1-x^2).
questions.topic: Second Derivatives of Inverse Trigonometric Functions

questions.q: If f(x) = cos^{-1}x, then the second derivative of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The second derivative of cos^{-1}x is x/√(1-x^2).
questions.topic: Second Derivatives of Inverse Trigonometric Functions

questions.q: If f(x) = tan^{-1}x, then the second derivative of f(x) is?
questions.explanation: The second derivative of tan^{-1}x is -2x/(1+x^2).
questions.topic: Second Derivatives of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
